revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 國柱命神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]岩科南側176-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 国柱姫命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 國柱命神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]岩科南側176-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 国柱姫命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月16日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=國柱命神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（[[1885年]]）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 国柱姫命
== 境内 == * [[クスノキ]] - 町指定天然記念物。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町 * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{DEFAULTSORT:くにはしらみことしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

| mapframe = yes

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 1 revision imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 國柱命神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]岩科南側176-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 国柱姫命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月16日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=國柱命神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（[[1885年]]）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 国柱姫命
== 境内 == * [[クスノキ]] - 町指定天然記念物。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町 * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{DEFAULTSORT:くにはしらみことしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

{{wikidata link|Q134893137}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|國柱命神社|version=103503024|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 國柱命神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]岩科南側176-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 国柱姫命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月16日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=國柱命神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、[[静岡県]][[賀茂郡]][[松崎町]]岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（[[1885年]]）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 国柱姫命
== 境内 == * [[クスノキ]] - 町指定天然記念物。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町 * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁
[[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]
==End Japanese==

{{translated page|ja|國柱命神社|version=103503024|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 國柱命神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側176-1 |位置 = {{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 国柱姫命 |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社}}、{{jalink|式内社}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月16日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=國柱命神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（{{jalink|1885年|Q7821}}）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 国柱姫命
== 境内 == * {{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}} - 町指定天然記念物。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町 * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁
==End Japanese== [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:松崎町の歴史]] [[Category:松崎町の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

'''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（{{jalink|1885年|Q7821}}）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱姫命

=== 境内 ===
* {{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}} - 町指定天然記念物。

[[JA:國柱命神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 國柱命神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側176-1
|位置   = {{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}

|創建   =

|神事   =

{{coord2|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（{{jalink|1885年|Q7821}}）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱姫命

=== 境内 ===
* {{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}} - 町指定天然記念物。

=== 脚注 ===

=== 外部リンク ===

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 國柱命神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側176-1
|位置   = {{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}

|創建   =

|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=國柱命神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（{{jalink|1885年|Q7821}}）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱姫命

=== 境内 ===
* {{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}} - 町指定天然記念物。

=== 脚注 ===

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 國柱命神社

|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側176-1

{{coord2|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（{{jalink|1885年|Q7821}}）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱姫命

=== 境内 ===
* {{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}} - 町指定天然記念物。

=== 脚注 ===

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（{{jalink|1885年|Q7821}}）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱姫命

=== 境内 ===
* {{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}} - 町指定天然記念物。

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（{{jalink|1885年|Q7821}}）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱姫命

=== 境内 ===
* {{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}} - 町指定天然記念物。

[[JA:國柱命神社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = 國柱命神社
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側176-1
|位置   = {{coord|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = 国柱姫命
|社格   = 旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、{{jalink|式内社}}
|創建   = 
|本殿   = 
|別名   =

|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=國柱命神社|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|44|5.9|N|138|46|38.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''國柱命神社'''（くにはしらみことじんじゃ、'''国柱命神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|賀茂郡|Q961511}}{{jalink|松崎町|Q1204017}}岩科南側（いわしななんそく）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
松崎町の南東、岩科地区を横断する岩科川の南側、岩科南側（なんそく）地区の中央の北西の山麓に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である国柱命神社（くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 国原姫の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - 松崎町</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

江戸時代以前には「岩科の宮」「神明宮」と称したが、明治18年（{{jalink|1885年|Q7821}}）に復称した<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 国柱姫命

=== 境内 ===
* {{jalink|クスノキ|Q158722}} - 町指定天然記念物。

=== 脚注 ===

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{jalink|1885|Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{jalink|Camphor tree|Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

{{Reflist}}

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{jalink|1885|Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{jalink|Camphor tree|Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{jalink|1885|Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{jalink|Camphor tree|Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Kunihashiramikoto Shrine
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}

|所在地 = {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}{{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}{{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}} Iwashina Nansoku 176-1

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{jalink|1885|Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{jalink|Camphor tree|Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{jalink|1885|Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{jalink|Camphor tree|Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{jalink|1885|Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{jalink|Camphor tree|Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{jalink|1885|Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{jalink|Camphor tree|Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{jalink|Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{jalink|1885|Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{jalink|Camphor tree|Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

{{Reflist}}

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}} - town-designated natural monument.

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|WD=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|WD=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|qid=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|qid=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===
* {{ill|lt=Camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} - town-designated natural monument.

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|qid=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|qid=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto

=== Precincts ===

{{nihongo|'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine'''|國柱命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Kunihashiramikoto Shrine''' (くにはしらみことじんじゃ, '''Kokubashiramikoto Shrine''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Iwashina Nansoku, {{ill|lt=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka|Q1204017|qid=Q1204017|1=Matsuzaki, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401152 國柱命神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northwestern mountain foot in the center of the Iwashina Nansoku district, on the south side of the Iwashina River that crosses the Iwashina area in southeastern Matsuzaki.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} of the same name, Kunihashiramikoto Shrine (くにのみはしらのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Kuniharahime no Myōjin"<ref name="town">[https://www.town.matsuzaki.shizuoka.jp/docs/2020120800012/ 国柱命神社] - Matsuzaki Town</ref><ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/kunihasira_title.htm 國柱命神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140314-01.html 国柱命神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

Before the Edo period it was called "Iwashina no Miya" and "Shinmei-gū," and in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|qid=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18) it resumed the current name<ref name="town"/><ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Kunihashirahime-no-Mikoto
